compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
CODE FIXES committed on branch worktree-jira-sync-automation (commit b23b3cce):
- FIX 2 (inbound scoping): fetcher.py JQL was hardcoded 'project = DIG'; now built from jira.project via jql_active/jql_done_recent/jqls_for; empty/invalid key fails closed. VERIFIED LIVE: reconcile-check unbound_jira 978 (DIG) -> 0 (REB).
- FIX 1 (outbound guard): applier._apply_batch pre-flight refuses (CrossProjectTargetError, fail-closed, no partial writes) any outbound update/delete targeting a project != configured jira.project. Creates + inbound exempt.
- Tests: +test_fetcher_project_scoped_jql.py, +test_applier_cross_project_guard.py (RED->GREEN). conftest autouse pins JIRA_PROJECT=DIG for legacy DIG-keyed tests; 2 DSO-keyed applier files pin DSO. Full reconciler suite 989 passed; integration 23 passed; ruff clean.
REMAINING — FIX 3 (re-target THIS store to REB): clear 62 DIG bindings (.bridge_state/bindings.json) + strip dso-id:jira-dig-* tags from local tickets + purge-bridge --keep=REB. This bulk-creates ~400 REB issues + rewrites the shared store, so it's a deliberate live cutover pending user confirmation.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
FIX 3 (re-target migration) BUILT + DRY-RUN VALIDATED on a clone copy (commit 71759efa) — live store UNTOUCHED:
- scripts/retarget_jira_project.py: clears bindings.json + prev_snapshot.json (with backup; optional --strip-tags), dry-run by default.
- Validated on a hardlinked clone of the DIG-bound store with the FIXED reconciler: dry-run plan went 1415 mutations (1017 DIG) -> after migration 398 outbound creates, 0 DIG. Every active ticket would re-create fresh in REB.
- docs/jira-sync-setup.md gains a 'Re-targeting an existing store' section.
Per user direction (build + dry-run first), the live cutover is NOT executed — awaiting review. A live pass before cutover safely REFUSES DIG (guard), so merging the schedule before cutover would just fail+alert; keep PR #20 auto-merge OFF until the live re-target is applied.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: RESOLVED + cutover complete. FIX 1 (cross-project guard) + FIX 2 (inbound REB-scoping) merged (#20). Re-target migration applied to live store (DIG bind-state cleared). ADF-over-length-description handling (#23 superseded by #25, ADF-aware fit) merged after live validation surfaced it. Live sync converged: 'OK: applied 404 of 404 mutations'; 355 REB bindings committed to origin/tickets; REB issues created with NO duplicates (the 154 from the first partial run were dedup-bound via rebar-id labels, not re-created); 0 DIG targets. Bridge now auto-syncing via */20 cron (recent passes succeed). Residual: status/parent/link sync converging over subsequent passes; non-Jira-user assignee churn tracked as follow-up 9b94 (floral-flute-sepal).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Closing: cross-project guard (FIX 1) + REB-scoped inbound fetch (FIX 2) merged via PR #20; over-length-description follow-up merged via PR #23 (9c875dd4, cites bug 626d); re-target migration applied to the live store (0 DIG targets, REB bindings committed). All acceptance criteria met per the RESOLVED comment above.

compiled_state.description:
## Reproduction Steps

1. A rebar store previously synced/bound to the **DIG** Jira project (bindings +
   `dig-####` rebar-id labels baked into event history).
2. Re-point sync at a different project: `rebar.toml [jira] project = "REB"`.
3. `rebar reconcile --mode dry-run`.

## Expected vs Actual

- **Expected:** with `jira.project = REB`, the reconciler syncs against REB only
  (or refuses, surfacing the project mismatch).
- **Actual:** the plan targets **DIG**: 1,018 / 1,415 planned mutations point at
  `DIG-####` keys (977 outbound UPDATEs + DIG-keyed creates); `reconcile-check`
  reports 978 unbound **DIG** issues on the inbound side even though REB is empty.
  Verified on navasage: DIG is the live DSO project (e.g. DIG-5805 is DSO's own
  canary bug) — a live pass would mutate real DSO issues.

## Root cause

`jira.project` only governs the CREATE project for *unbound* tickets. Tickets
already bound (binding store `.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json`:
62 confirmed DIG bindings) or carrying `dig-####` labels keep targeting DIG. There
is no cross-project guard and no supported "re-target to a new project" migration.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The reconciler refuses (or loudly warns + skips) when a planned outbound
      mutation targets a Jira key whose project ≠ configured `jira.project`
      (cross-project safety guard, fail-closed).
- [ ] The inbound fetch is scoped to `jira.project` (REB), not the whole instance / DIG.
- [ ] A documented path to re-target an existing store to a new project (clear/rebind
      bind-state + strip stale rebar-id labels), so a clean REB mirror can be built.
- [ ] Re-running the dry-run after migration shows 0 mutations targeting DIG.
compiled_state.title: Reconciler targets DIG despite jira.project=REB — stale DIG bindings/labels + no cross-project guard
